entries.title: CATENA on james 1:22
entries.excerpt: In other words, be mindful of your own salvation!

entries.title: SERMONS 179.7-8 on james 1:22
entries.excerpt: Neither I nor any other preacher can see into your hearts … but God is looking, for nothing can be hidden from him.… Do not deceive yourselves by coming eagerly to hear the Word and then failing to do it. If it is a good thing to hear, it is a much better thing to do. If you do not hear, you cannot do, and therefore you will build nothing. But if you hear and do not do, then what you are building will be a ruin.

entries.title: SERMONS 71.22 on james 1:22
entries.excerpt: James did not say "of the words" but "of the Word," in spite of the fact that there are so many words from the Holy Scriptures which are venerated in the church.

entries.title: Commentary on the Catholic Epistles on james 1:22
entries.excerpt: Be however doers of the word, and not hearers only, etc. Thus also Paul about the observers of the law. "Not the hearers," he says, "of the law are just before God, but the doers of the law shall be justified" (Rom. II). And in the Apocalypse John, having said: "Blessed is he who reads and those who hear the words of the prophecy of this book," immediately added: "And keep those things which are written therein" (Apoc. I).

entries.title: The Desert Fathers, Sayings of the Early Christian Monks on james 1:22
entries.excerpt: Three brothers once came to a hermit in Scetis. One of them said to him, 'Abba, I have memorized the Old and New Testaments.' But the hermit answered, 'And you have filled the air with words.' The second said to him, 'I have written out the Old and New Testaments with my own hand.' But the hermit said, 'And you have filled the window-ledge with manuscripts.' The third said, 'The grass is growing up my chimney.' But the hermit answered, 'And you have driven away hospitality.'

entries.title: The Desert Fathers, Sayings of the Early Christian Monks on james 1:22
entries.excerpt: Another brother spoke with the same Theodore, and he began to talk about matters of which he had no experience. Theodore said to him, 'You've not yet found a ship to sail in, nor put your luggage aboard, nor put out to sea, and you're already acting as if you were in the city which you mean to reach. If you make some attempt to do the things you are discussing, then you can talk about them with understanding.'

entries.title: Commentary on James on james 1:22
entries.excerpt:
But be doers of the word, and not hearers only, deceiving yourselves.
Since James knows that some who listen to the word with diligence often extinguish their fervor even at the very time they are listening, therefore, he now adds this, commanding it in effect: Do not show diligence only in hearing, but much more in doing.

entries.title: Commentary on James on james 1:22
entries.excerpt: "Deceiving yourselves" means deluding yourselves, counting your own salvation as nothing.
